chunks.en: Today I want to talk about how I get to work.
chunks.ja: 今日は私がどうやって仕事に行くかについて話したいです。

chunks.en: I usually leave home at eight in the morning.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい朝8時に家を出ます。

chunks.en: I take the train to the office every day.
chunks.ja: 私は毎日電車でオフィスに行きます。

chunks.en: I walk to the station for about ten minutes.
chunks.ja: 駅までは10分ほど歩きます。

chunks.en: On the way I listen to music on my phone.
chunks.ja: 移動中はスマホで音楽を聴きます。

chunks.en: Sometimes I read the news on my phone too.
chunks.ja: 時々スマホでニュースも読みます。

chunks.en: It takes about thirty minutes to get there.
chunks.ja: そこに着くまで約30分かかります。

chunks.en: The train is often crowded in the morning.
chunks.ja: 電車は朝よくとても混んでいます。

outline: Opening: how I get to work
Point 1: when I leave and which train I take
Point 2: what I do on the way

modelTalk.text: I usually leave home at half past seven. I don't like to rush in the morning. Most days I take the train to work. The station is close to my home. So I just walk there. It's about five minutes. On the way I listen to music. Sometimes I look at my phone too. I read the news or send messages. The trip takes about thirty minutes. In the morning the train is really full. It's hard to find a seat. But I don't mind much. I like this quiet time before work. When it's warm, I walk part of the way. That's how I get to work.

chunks.en: I usually leave home at eight in the morning.
chunks.ja: 私はいつも朝8時に家を出ます。

chunks.en: I take the train to my office every day.
chunks.ja: 私は毎日電車で会社に行きます。

chunks.en: The train is very crowded in the morning.
chunks.ja: 電車は朝とても混んでいます。

chunks.en: On the way I listen to music on my phone.
chunks.ja: 移動中は携帯で音楽を聴きます。

chunks.en: Sometimes I read the news on my phone too.
chunks.ja: ときどき携帯でニュースも読みます。

chunks.en: It takes about thirty minutes to get there.
chunks.ja: そこに着くまで約30分かかります。

chunks.en: So my trip to work is easy and simple.
chunks.ja: だから私の通勤は楽で簡単です。

outline: Point 1: train and how crowded it is
Point 2: what I do on the way
Wrap-up: total time and how easy it is

modelTalk.text: I usually leave home at eight o'clock. I don't like to be late. I walk to the train station first. It's about ten minutes on foot. Then I take the train to work. The train is often crowded in the morning. Sometimes I can't find a seat. On the way, I listen to music. I like slow songs in the morning. Sometimes I look at my phone too. I read the news or check messages. The whole trip takes about thirty minutes. It's not too long, so I'm okay. When I get there, I feel ready. That's how I start my day.
